sections.executive_summary: A 10-reviewer review panel ran against `main` @ b7a44b8 and produced 100+ findings clustered into 12 implementation branches across 4 stages. Two Stage 1 foundation branches (`fix/data-schema-and-validation` and `fix/engine-di-seams`) are already built and verified. This PRD covers the 10 remaining branches; each user story corresponds to one branch with verifiable acceptance criteria for autonomous execution.
sections.problem_statement: Recent feature work shipped a richer stat block and 5.2 SRD data refresh; a multi-perspective review surfaced systemic issues across accessibility, build pipeline correctness, engine testability, and operational hygiene. Many findings are individually small but cluster into coherent themes. Without a planned, sequenced effort the work either stalls or risks shipping partial fixes that interact poorly. The two completed Stage 1 branches established schema-version contracts and engine DI seams that the remaining branches build on.
sections.goals_and_success_metrics: Primary goals: close every HIGH-severity finding; establish a UI test layer; cut initial bundle weight by code-splitting the monster catalog; pass an automated accessibility audit on key flows. Success metrics: 100% of HIGH findings closed; initial JS bundle under 50 KB gzip pre-roll; prefers-reduced-motion honoured; 70+ tests including component and e2e; validate:data step in CI on every push; service-worker kill switch versioned and tested.
sections.scope_in_and_out: In scope: every branch listed in the roadmap's Stages 1 (remaining), 2, 3, and 4; new tests required by changed code; drive-by lint or formatting fixes that block CI; updates to validate:data and ci.yml when needed. Out of scope: visual identity refresh beyond style/visual-polish; new product features; framework or hosting migration; re-fetching monsters.json from Open5e; future-roadmap +data and +l10n opt-in panels; remote pushes or merges to main.
sections.risks_and_mitigations: A11y semantics may break the segmented control's visual styling (mitigation: land a11y-semantics before visual-polish, screenshot smoke after each). Code-split chunk may not serve correctly via Cloudflare static assets (mitigation: npm run build + preview locally before merging, add a smoke test). SW kill-switch cache shape change may evict legitimate state on first visit (mitigation: LRU and version tests, fresh-profile manual smoke). CSP may block legitimate resources (mitigation: try Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only first, review browser console). Loop may mark a story passed without ACs being met (mitigation: ACs written for autonomous verification; skill must not mark passed without evidence).
sections.open_questions: Q1. Should observability logs be enabled by default in prod or gated behind ?debug=1? Default: gated. Q2. Can the segmented control revert to native radios with native focus rings? Default: yes. Q3. Replace vendor emoji with custom SVG icons or simply remove them? Default: remove with a placeholder note. Q4. SW kill switch: block this visit or unregister on next? Default: serve from cache this visit, unregister next. Q5. CSP header location: static/_headers or SvelteKit handle hook? Default: static/_headers.

stories.title: fix/state-and-storage-validation
stories.capability: localStorage payloads validated before merge, the campfire $effect moved to the state owner, isComplete derived rather than re-called, and a mockable newSeed seam
stories.outcome: state corruption can't bypass the form's all-complete guard and tests can deterministically pin the seed stream
acceptance_criteria: A new validator (or reuse of parseInputs-style helper) rejects localStorage payloads that don't match the input schema; failed validation drops the payload silently and leaves in-memory state untouched.
The v1 storage key (fourth-watch-inputs-v1, if present) is cleared on first run after the migration; only v2 is read going forward.
The campfire auto-clear $effect lives in +page.svelte (state owner), not InputForm.svelte; InputForm.svelte no longer writes back through $bindable.
isComplete(inputs) is consumed via $derived(...), not called inline in the template.
A new module wraps Math.random() so tests can supply a deterministic generator; +page.svelte uses the wrapper.
New tests cover: tampered storage payload is rejected; valid storage payload is loaded; the seed wrapper is injectable.
Smoke test: open dev server, load with valid stored input, refresh, see the previous selections; corrupt localStorage manually, refresh, see no crash and a fresh form.
Pipeline gates pass: npm run check, npm run lint, npm run validate:data, npm test all green.

stories.title: feat/test-coverage-uplift
stories.capability: boundary tests for the engine, schema-shape contract tests for the bundled data files, and a component / e2e test layer
stories.outcome: UI regressions are caught and engine edge cases are pinned to behaviour
acceptance_criteria: Engine boundary tests added: partyLevel=1, partyLevel=20, partySize=1, partySize=8; the all-zero category-weights path; the encounter-happens-pool-empty branch; the weather forced-Light fallback.
Direct tests for pickIndex, pickFrom, rollD100 against empty / all-zero / single-element inputs.
Schema-shape contract tests run monsters.json, encounter-modifiers.json, climate-weather.json through the runtime parser or hand-written shape checks.
The 'never Hot+Heavy' weather invariant test re-rigged with climate=Tropical season=Summer so the guard actually fires.
Vitest configured to run a subset of tests in jsdom (e.g. tests/components/**); component smoke tests exist for Stepper, InputForm, ResultPanel, EncounterBlock.
One Playwright e2e covers the happy path: pick five settings, roll, see a result, click Copy, verify seed is in clipboard.
npm test runs all the above in under 30s on a developer laptop; CI runs them too.
Pipeline gates pass: npm run check, npm run lint, npm run validate:data, npm test all green.

stories.title: fix/a11y-focus-and-motion
stories.user_role: keyboard or switch user, or a user with motion-sensitivity
stories.capability: visible focus indicators on every interactive control and animations that respect my reduced-motion preference
stories.outcome: I can use the app without losing track of focus or being made dizzy
acceptance_criteria: :focus-visible styles applied to .seg and .pad so the wrapper shows a focus ring when the inner hidden input is focused.
Global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block in app.css disables transforms, the radial body gradient, and hover translations on .seg, .pad, button.roll, .expand, ghost buttons.
.seg and .pad min-height bumped to ~44pt; the stack gap loosened.
Visually-hidden <input> pattern replaced with clip-path: inset(50%) sr-only.
Manual smoke: tab through the form, every control shows a visible ring; reduce motion in OS settings, reload, animations are disabled.
Pipeline gates pass: npm run check, npm run lint, npm run validate:data, npm test all green.

stories.title: fix/a11y-semantics-and-aria
stories.capability: the segmented control, stat-block disclosure, stepper, and live region announced correctly
stories.outcome: the app is intelligible without sight
acceptance_criteria: Segmented Travelling/At camp control rebuilt with native <input type='radio'> + <label> (no pointer-events: none) or with a roving-tabindex + arrow-key handler; choice documented.
role='table' removed from EncounterBlock.svelte:93; replaced with a real <table> or <dl>.
Stat-block disclosure has an id on the article and aria-controls on the toggle.
Stepper exposes role='spinbutton' with aria-valuenow/min/max; the redundant aria-live on the value span is removed.
+page.svelte aria-live region wraps a short status string, not the entire ResultPanel.
Rotate glyphs on reroll buttons are aria-hidden.
Emoji removed from <select> <option> text (or option aria-label set without it).
MechanicsChip carries an icon or text prefix per severity, not background color alone.
Disabled campfire pad has aria-disabled='true' on the visible target.
Em-dash in <title> replaced with colon or pipe.
Seed <code> tied to its label via aria-describedby.
New tests verify: stepper announces its value; segmented control responds to arrow keys (or whichever path was taken); live region only re-announces the status string.
Pipeline gates pass: npm run check, npm run lint, npm run validate:data, npm test all green.

stories.title: perf/code-split-monsters-and-fonts
stories.user_role: tabletop user opening the app on mobile
stories.capability: the page paints quickly without waiting on a 545 KB monster catalog
acceptance_criteria: roll() no longer eagerly imports monstersData; the catalog is loaded via dynamic await import() from +layout.ts load or behind the splash on +page.svelte mount.
Cinzel and Inter self-hosted (woff2 subsets) or preloaded via <link rel='preconnect'> + <link rel='preload'>; render-blocking @import in app.css gone.
formatNumberMap(c.savingThrows) and formatNumberMap(c.skills) in EncounterBlock.svelte hoisted via $derived.
npm run build succeeds; the initial JS chunk no longer contains monster names (verifiable by grep).
Smoke test: refresh dev server, splash renders before the catalog loads (or simulate slow 3G and verify form is interactive before catalog finishes).
Existing tests still pass; roll() defaults to the bundled catalog when called synchronously.
Pipeline gates pass: npm run check, npm run lint, npm run validate:data, npm test all green.

stories.title: feat/form-ux-feedback
stories.capability: persistent labels, an explanation when Roll is disabled, copy-button feedback, primary-action consistency, a disclosure chevron, a campfire hint, and a faster Stepper
stories.outcome: the path from open-app to see-result is obvious and forgiving
acceptance_criteria: Setting select labels visible above each control; the placeholder option is no longer the only label carrier.
Inline hint near the disabled Roll button names the missing fields.
Copy button switches its label to 'Copied' briefly with an aria-live status announcement; clipboard rejection shows a transient error.
Primary-action layout consistent: Roll-again either matches the chunky Roll button styling or the original button transforms into Roll-again. Choice documented.
Show-stat-block toggle has a chevron that rotates on aria-expanded.
Disabled campfire pad shows an '(unlocks at camp)' caption.
Stepper has both an editable number input and +/- buttons with hold-to-repeat (interval on pointerdown, cleared on pointerup/pointerleave).
New component tests: copy-button feedback, stepper editable-input parity, hint-while-disabled, chevron rotation.
Smoke test: walking the happy path feels faster than before.
Pipeline gates pass: npm run check, npm run lint, npm run validate:data, npm test all green.

stories.title: style/visual-polish
stories.capability: polished visual hierarchy with one primary action, themed icons in place of vendor emoji, and consistent typography
stories.outcome: the table presence matches the engine quality
acceptance_criteria: Affirmative state text or weight added to noise/campfire pads.
Reroll bar restructured: primary Roll-again gets chunky styling; per-stream rerolls become secondary ghost buttons.
H1 cap reduced or breathing room added so legends don't compete.
Mobile Roll button has min-width and justify-self below 640px viewport.
Vendor emoji in selects replaced with themed inline SVG icons or stripped to plain text.
Dead grid-template-columns: 9.5rem 1fr rule removed.
Stat-block lead-ins use one emphasis (italic or bold), not both.
Chip border alpha bumped or warn/danger backgrounds tinted higher so chip edge is visible.
Label tier count reduced to two per fieldset.
Stepper +/- glyph weight matches the value digit weight.
Result panel hairline border decision: bumped alpha or removed.
Cinzel use restricted to >=0.85rem; legends below that size use Inter smallcaps.
static/favicon.svg carries a gold accent glint or other brand element.
Smoke test: side-by-side the app vs. pre-polish screenshots, the visual hierarchy reads cleaner.
Pipeline gates pass: npm run check, npm run lint, npm run validate:data, npm test all green.

stories.title: feat/service-worker-killswitch
stories.capability: a service-worker kill switch and a bounded runtime cache
stories.outcome: a bad deploy can't ship a broken cached shell that survives the next visit
acceptance_criteria: A versioned kill-switch constant in source; when bumped, activate calls self.registration.unregister() and clears all caches.
Runtime cache bounded: max N entries with LRU eviction, max-age on cached responses.
cache.put rejection inside respondWith caught and swallowed with a debug log.
New tests run the SW with a mock Cache interface and verify: kill-switch bump triggers unregister; LRU evicts oldest; cache.put rejection doesn't crash the handler.
Manual smoke: install the SW in dev, bump the kill-switch version, reload, verify the SW is unregistered and the app is served fresh.
Pipeline gates pass: npm run check, npm run lint, npm run validate:data, npm test all green.

stories.title: feat/observability
stories.capability: a way to reproduce a roll from the seed
stories.outcome: I can inspect the exact result the user saw
acceptance_criteria: Every roll() invocation logs seed, top-level inputs, and a result digest to console.info; gated by an env or build-time flag so production console can stay quiet by default.
An optional Cloudflare-Pages-side error-reporter hook is documented but not wired in by default.
New component test asserts the log line shape; smoke test confirms the seed is recoverable from the console log on a real roll.
Decision documented in docs/superpowers/plans/: are we shipping this to prod by default, or only enabled via a query string for support?
Pipeline gates pass: npm run check, npm run lint, npm run validate:data, npm test all green.

stories.title: chore/security-defense-in-depth
stories.capability: a Content-Security-Policy header and a dependency-audit step in CI
stories.outcome: an XSS pivot has no script-eval surface and a known-CVE bump doesn't ship silently
acceptance_criteria: A _headers file at the static root with at minimum: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'; Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin; a Permissions-Policy baseline.
CSP doesn't break self-hosted fonts (from story 5) and doesn't break Cloudflare's analytics if any are wired up.
.github/workflows/ci.yml gains either an npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high step or a dependency-review-action step; choice documented.
Smoke test: dev server still works with the CSP applied; build still works; CI still passes; deliberately introducing a known-vulnerable dep makes CI fail with a clear message.
Pipeline gates pass: npm run check, npm run lint, npm run validate:data, npm test all green.
